creator: raimeetravel
caption:
Places no one talks about in TOKYO! If you want to explore some things a little different from the typical recommendations, this is for you! 

I have been to Tokyo many times, but it was my first time doing each of these things and it was a great way to experience a different side of the city! 

Comment PATH and I’ll send you my blog post with my entire off-the-beaten-path Japan itinerary!

@tokyotokyooldmeetsnew 
#Japan #Tokyo #tokyotokyo
#2025connectedjapan

transcription.text: Let's get off the beaten path in Tokyo. I pretty much always see the same suggestions, but we're not doing any of those. Here are some things you've probably never seen before. I've been to Tokyo 10 times now, and these were all new to me, so let's get into it. First, go check out the Hanazono Shrine. This is a shrine in the center of the city. It's very cool, surrounded by buildings, but you'll feel like you're stepping back in time. Then, walk around this area before getting some delicious unagi for lunch. This restaurant was so good. Then, this was my favorite part of the day. We did a tour of the National Stadium where the Olympics were held. I would love to see an event here, too. Then, you'll round out your day shopping around these two areas. There's cute shops and food if you wanna grab a snack, but then you're going to get dinner at this spot, which was incredible. Okay, I have to do a part two because I have even more off the beaten path Tokyo suggestions, so stay tuned.
